title: Weighted Mazes, Dijkstra, Heaps, and A* · Behavior Case: Dijkstra Relaxation
summary: The heuristic overestimates, heap priorities are mutated in place, stale entries finalize wrong costs, and geometric distance ignores movement rules. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dijkstra relaxation increment in weighted Dijkstra and A-star comparison engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relax an edge only through a lower tentative cost and finalize vertices under the nonnegative-weight contract.
Define nonnegative passage costs, units, blocked edges, accumulated route cost, and the player meaning of weight.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-weighted-dijkstra-astar-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-weighted-dijkstra-astar-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_weighted_dijkstra_astar_5(reported_cost: int = 27, oracle_cost: int = 27, expansions: int = 34, stale_entries_skipped: int = 6, heuristic_admissible: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if reported_cost < 0 or oracle_cost < 0 or expansions < 1:
        return False, reported_cost, "weighted-count"
    if reported_cost != oracle_cost:
        return False, reported_cost, "nonoptimal-cost"
    if stale_entries_skipped < 0:
        return False, stale_entries_skipped, "heap-accounting"
    if not heuristic_admissible:
        return False, expansions, "heuristic-overestimate"
    assert reported_cost == oracle_cost
    return True, reported_cost, "optimal-weighted-route"

Reconstruct the conditions for Dijkstra Relaxation, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Dijkstra Relaxation model · Theory Dijkstra Relaxation
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Dijkstra Relaxation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory puzzle team handles case 4295 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate weighted relaxation, heap staleness, heuristic limits, route cost, and optimality; the player cannot rely on color or animation; then change the seed and retain replay identity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Relax an edge only through a lower tentative cost and finalize vertices under the nonnegative-weight contract.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Dijkstra Relaxation model · Theory Dijkstra Relaxation.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Dijkstra Relaxation model · Theory Dijkstra Relaxation, keep Dijkstra Relaxation connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Relax an edge only through a lower tentative cost and finalize vertices under the nonnegative-weight contract.
